subsystem: PHP EXIF extension value-decoder. `exif_iif_add_value()` is the helper in ext/exif/exif.c that takes a single decoded EXIF/TIFF IFD tag (tag id, TIFF format code, element count `length`, and a raw pointer `value` into the parsed image bytes) and copies/decodes it into the process-side `image_info` structure that later feeds exif_read_data(). It is on the untrusted-image parse path.
provenance: Identified from PHP/Zend idioms: the `TSRMLS_DC`/`TSRMLS_CC` thread-safety macros, Zend memory allocators `safe_erealloc`/`safe_emalloc`/`estrdup`/`estrndup`, the `php_error_docref` logging macro, the EXIF-specific accessors `php_ifd_get16u/32u/16s/32s`, the `php_tiff_bytes_per_format[]` table, and the `TAG_FMT_*` / `TAG_MAKER_NOTE` constants. The header comment `/* sample: primevul_c_train_3224_vuln */` and the file name `train_0135_cwe125.c` mark it as a PrimeVul sample flagged for CWE-125 (out-of-bounds read). This is the PHP EXIF `exif_iif_add_value` function.
trust_boundary: The `value` pointer and the `length`, `format`, and `tag` integers all originate from an attacker-supplied image file (JPEG/TIFF EXIF blocks) parsed upstream by exif_process_IFD_TAG. An attacker fully controls the TIFF `format` code, the element `count` that becomes `length`, and the byte offset/extent that `value` points at inside the mmap'd/heap image buffer. Nothing in this function re-validates that `value` actually has `length` * per-format-bytes readable bytes behind it; the only local sanity gate is `length < 0`. Everything reachable through `value`/`length`/`format` is therefore hostile.

what_the_code_does.code:
/* sample: primevul_c_train_3224_vuln */
static void exif_iif_add_value(image_info_type *image_info, int section_index, char *name, int tag, int format, int length, void* value, int motorola_intel TSRMLS_DC)
what_the_code_does.explanation: Declaration of the internal helper that records one decoded EXIF tag into `image_info`. Parameters: `image_info` is the accumulator struct; `section_index` selects which IFD section list (main, thumbnail, GPS, etc.) the value is appended to; `name` is the tag's human name; `tag` is the numeric EXIF tag id; `format` is the TIFF data-format code (one of the `TAG_FMT_*` values) that decides how `value` is interpreted; `length` is the element COUNT for that format (not a byte length for the numeric types); `value` is a raw pointer into the already-parsed image bytes; `motorola_intel` is the byte-order flag passed to the `php_ifd_get*` accessors; `TSRMLS_DC` is the Zend thread-safety context. Of these, `section_index`, `tag`, `format`, `length`, and `value` derive from attacker-controlled image data.

Locals. `idex` is the element loop index (unsigned size_t). `vptr` is a moving cursor into `value` used when iterating the numeric array elements. `info_value` points at the destination union (`image_info_value`) that stores the decoded scalar/string/list. `info_data` points at the per-tag record just appended to the section list. `list` temporarily holds the reallocated array so the original pointer is not lost on realloc failure.

The only local input gate: a negative element count is rejected. Because `length` is a signed `int`, this rejects values with the sign bit set but does nothing to bound large positive values (up to INT_MAX). It also does not relate `length` to the actual number of readable bytes behind `value`, so it prevents negative-index math but not over-reads.

Grows the section's array of `image_info_data` records by one element using `safe_erealloc`, the Zend overflow-checked reallocator (it validates `(count+1)*sizeof(...)` for multiplication overflow and aborts on OOM, so the allocation size itself is guarded). The result is assigned to a temporary `list` first and then stored back, which correctly avoids the classic `p = realloc(p, ...)` pointer-loss bug. Note `section_index` is used unchecked as an index into `info_list[]` here and again below.

Points `info_data` at the newly grown slot (index = current `count`), zero-initializes it, then stores the tag id, format, and element length, duplicates the tag name with `estrdup`, and takes the address of the embedded `value` union into `info_value`. `info_data->length` is provisionally set to the raw `length`; several format branches below recompute it. `info_value` is the destination for the decoded payload.

String branch. When `value` is non-NULL it calls `php_strnlen(value, length)` to find the string length bounded by `length`, then `estrndup(value, length)` to copy that many bytes into a newly allocated NUL-terminated PHP string, and records the (possibly shortened) length. `php_strnlen` scans up to `length` bytes looking for a NUL, so both the scan and the copy read up to `length` bytes from `value`. If `value` is NULL an empty string is stored.

Unknown formats fall into `default`, which rewrites `info_data->tag` to `TAG_FMT_UNDEFINED` and then FALLS THROUGH (no break) into the signed/unsigned BYTE cases, which also fall through into `TAG_FMT_UNDEFINED` below. The byte cases short-circuit only when `length` is zero. So all of default, SBYTE, BYTE, and UNDEFINED share the raw-copy logic at lines 49-62 with `length` treated as a byte count.

The raw-bytes branch for UNDEFINED/BYTE/SBYTE/unknown formats. For the special MAKER_NOTE tag, `length` is clamped to `strlen(value)` (which itself scans `value` until a NUL, an unbounded read). For every other tag the comment explicitly says `do not recompute length here`, and `estrndup(value, length)` copies exactly `length` attacker-chosen bytes out of the image buffer with NO bound tying `length` to the readable size behind `value`. This is the central over-read surface.

The numeric/array branch. Here `length` is an element COUNT, not a byte count. Zero length breaks out. For more than one element it allocates an array of `length` `image_info_value` unions via `safe_emalloc` (overflow-checked). For a single element it decodes directly into the embedded union. The allocation is size-safe, but nothing here checks that the SOURCE buffer `value` actually holds `length` elements worth of bytes.

Iterates `length` times, advancing `vptr` by `php_tiff_bytes_per_format[format]` bytes each step (2 for shorts, 4 for longs/singles, 8 for rationals/doubles). Each iteration decodes one element from `vptr` using the byte-order-aware accessors: `php_ifd_get16u` reads 2 bytes, `php_ifd_get32u` reads 4. URATIONAL reads TWO 32-bit words — the denominator at `4+(char *)vptr`, i.e. 8 bytes per element. The source cursor `vptr` walks forward across the image buffer without any end-of-buffer check; correctness depends entirely on the caller having verified that `length * bytes_per_format` bytes are readable behind `value`.

Signed shorts/longs decode like their unsigned counterparts; SRATIONAL again reads 8 bytes (num + den at `4+(char *)vptr`). The floating branches are anomalous: SINGLE reads `*(float *)value` (4 bytes) and — critically — has NO `break;`, so it falls through into DOUBLE, which reads `*(double *)value` (8 bytes). Both float and double read from the base pointer `value`, NOT from the moving cursor `vptr`, so multi-element float/double arrays repeatedly decode the first element, and the missing break makes a SINGLE tag also perform an 8-byte double read.

After decoding, marks the section as present in the `sections_found` bitmask by shifting `1` left by `section_index`, and increments the section's element `count` so the next call appends after this record. `1<<section_index` is computed in `int`; a `section_index` >= 31 would be undefined shift behavior, and a large/negative `section_index` would also corrupt the `info_list[]` indexing used earlier.

what_could_go_wrong.explanation: Primary out-of-bounds read. For TAG_FMT_UNDEFINED/BYTE/SBYTE (and any unknown format routed through `default`), `estrndup(value, length)` copies exactly `length` bytes from `value`, and the comment deliberately forbids re-deriving `length` from the real data extent. `length` is the attacker-controlled EXIF component count/byte-count, bounded only by `length >= 0` (line 10) — up to INT_MAX. If the crafted tag declares a `length` larger than the bytes actually remaining behind `value` in the parsed image buffer, `estrndup` reads past the buffer end and copies adjacent heap memory into a PHP string that exif_read_data() returns to script — an information-disclosure over-read (and potential crash). The implementation MUST validate `length <= (bytes available behind value)` before this copy; upstream IFD parsing is the only place that could enforce it.

Missing `break` after the SINGLE case makes it fall through into DOUBLE. A tag legitimately of format TAG_FMT_SINGLE carries only 4 bytes per element (per `php_tiff_bytes_per_format`), but the fall-through then executes `*(double *)value`, dereferencing 8 bytes at `value`. When `value` points at the last 4 bytes of the image buffer, this reads 4 bytes past the end (CWE-125). Additionally both reads use `value` (the base) rather than `vptr`, so the over-read is at a fixed location and element indexing is wrong for arrays. The invariant: SINGLE must stop after its 4-byte read, and reads must use `vptr` with a bounds check against buffer end.

Array over-read for the numeric formats. The loop advances `vptr` across `length` elements of `php_tiff_bytes_per_format[format]` bytes each and calls `php_ifd_get16u/get32u/get16s/get32s` at each position, but never compares `vptr` against the end of the image buffer. An attacker sets `length` far larger than the bytes actually present for the tag, and the loop walks `vptr` off the end, reading `length * bytes_per_format` bytes of adjacent memory into the decoded value list that is returned to userland. URATIONAL/SRATIONAL (`4+(char *)vptr`) read 8 bytes per element, doubling the reach. The implementation MUST bound `length * bytes_per_format` against the readable source extent before the loop.

String over-read. `php_strnlen(value, length)` scans up to `length` bytes of `value` searching for a NUL terminator. If the attacker sets `length` larger than the bytes available and there is no NUL before the buffer end, the scan (and the subsequent `estrndup` copy) reads past the buffer boundary (CWE-125). Unlike the raw-bytes branch there is a NUL short-circuit, which usually limits exposure, but a buffer lacking an embedded NUL still over-reads up to `length`. The safe invariant is the same: `length` must be clamped to the readable source size before scanning.

For the MAKER_NOTE tag the code calls `strlen(value)` on an attacker-controlled pointer to clamp `length`. `strlen` reads until it finds a NUL byte; if the maker-note payload is not NUL-terminated within the buffer, `strlen` itself runs off the end of the image buffer (CWE-125) before the `MIN` can help. The clamp is intended as a safety measure but introduces its own unbounded scan. The implementation must instead clamp against a known byte extent, not scan for a terminator that hostile input may omit.

The lone input gate only rejects negative `length`; it lets any positive `int` through. Downstream `length` is cast to `(size_t)length` (line 80) for the element loop and used directly as a byte count in the estrndup branches, so a large positive `length` (e.g. up to INT_MAX) sails through and drives every over-read described above. The gate should also reject `length` that exceeds the bytes available behind `value`; without the buffer extent being passed in, this function cannot enforce that invariant at all — evidence the safety contract lives in the (not-provided) caller.

`section_index` indexes `info_list[]` (and `1<<section_index` at line 126) with no local bounds check. In normal EXIF flow `section_index` is one of a small fixed set of section constants chosen by the caller, so this is latent rather than directly attacker-driven, but if any caller ever forwards an image-derived or out-of-range section index it becomes an out-of-bounds array access / write. The implementation must guarantee `0 <= section_index < SECTION_COUNT` and `< 31` for the shift.

summary: Attacker-controlled EXIF tags drive `exif_iif_add_value`: the element count/byte-length `length` and the source pointer `value` come straight from a crafted image, and the function copies/decodes `length` units out of `value` with only a `length < 0` check and no verification that `value` actually holds that many bytes. This yields several heap out-of-bounds reads (CWE-125) — the raw `estrndup(value, length)` copy, the numeric element loop, the `php_strnlen`/`strlen` scans, and a missing `break` that turns a 4-byte SINGLE read into an 8-byte DOUBLE read — that leak adjacent process memory into script-visible EXIF output. The real bound must be enforced by the (not-provided) IFD parser that supplies `value`/`length`.

reviewed_anchors.reason: Takes attacker-derived length/value/format/tag with no buffer-extent parameter; the whole over-read surface.
Attacker-controlled element/byte count, only gated by length<0; drives every over-read.
Raw pointer into image buffer; read for `length` units with no end check.
Selects decode path and indexes php_tiff_bytes_per_format[]; controls per-element byte stride.
TAG_MAKER_NOTE branch triggers an unbounded strlen(value) scan.
Unchecked index into info_list[] and shift amount at line 126; latent OOB if caller-forwarded out of range.
Byte-order flag passed to accessors; no memory-safety contract of its own.
estrdup copies a NUL-terminated tag name from a trusted table; not attacker-length-controlled.
Thread-safety context plumbing; no security contract.
Insufficient gate: rejects only negatives, permits INT_MAX-scale lengths and does not relate length to buffer size.
Overflow-checked Zend reallocator assigned via temporary; allocation itself is safe.
Overflow-checked allocator sized to length*sizeof(union); destination size is safe (source read is the risk).
Zeroes the freshly allocated fixed-size record; bounded and safe.
Scans and copies up to attacker `length` bytes from value; over-read if no NUL before buffer end.
Routes unknown formats into the UNDEFINED raw-copy path; the over-read is captured by the UNDEFINED finding.
Zero-length short-circuit only; falls through to the UNDEFINED estrndup finding.
strlen on hostile unterminated buffer over-reads before MIN clamps.
Copies exactly attacker `length` bytes with an explicit 'do not recompute' comment; primary CWE-125.
Walks vptr across length elements with no buffer-end check; array over-read.
Each dereferences 2/4 bytes of vptr; combined with unbounded loop they read past buffer.
Reads 8 bytes per element, doubling the loop's reach past the source buffer.
Fall-through into DOUBLE performs an 8-byte read for a 4-byte format; CWE-125.
Read from base `value` not `vptr`; over-reads at buffer end and mis-indexes arrays.
Writes into the safe_emalloc'd destination list of length elements; destination is correctly sized.
int shift by section_index; UB / corruption if section_index >= 31 or out of range (same anchor as section_index).
Increments the per-section counter after a successful append; correct bookkeeping.

findings.title: Out-of-bounds read: estrndup copies attacker-chosen `length` bytes from `value` (UNDEFINED/BYTE)
findings.bug_class: Heap out-of-bounds read (unchecked length vs source buffer)
findings.where_it_lives: corpus/train/train_0135_cwe125.c (over-read); bound enforcement in implementation (caller/IFD parser file not provided)
findings.invariant: `length` must be <= the number of bytes actually readable behind `value` for this tag; the copy must never exceed the parsed component's extent in the image buffer.
findings.failure_mode: A crafted EXIF tag of format UNDEFINED/BYTE/SBYTE (or any unknown format routed through `default`) declares `length` larger than the bytes present for it. With only `length < 0` checked and the comment forbidding recomputation, `estrndup(value, length)` reads past the end of the image buffer and copies adjacent heap memory into a PHP string returned by exif_read_data(), disclosing process memory (and possibly crashing on an unmapped page).
findings.what_to_confirm: That the caller passes a `length` already clamped to the remaining bytes behind `value`, and that no path lets an image-declared component count reach this estrndup unbounded.

findings.title: Out-of-bounds read: numeric element loop walks `value` for `length` elements with no end check
findings.bug_class: Heap out-of-bounds read (array iteration past source buffer)
findings.where_it_lives: corpus/train/train_0135_cwe125.c (loop); source-extent check in implementation (file not provided)
findings.invariant: `length * php_tiff_bytes_per_format[format]` must not exceed the readable bytes behind `value`; the loop must stop at the buffer end.
findings.failure_mode: For USHORT/ULONG/URATIONAL/SSHORT/SLONG/SRATIONAL the loop advances `vptr` by 2/4/8 bytes per element and calls the `php_ifd_get*` accessors, but never checks `vptr` against buffer end. An attacker sets `length` far beyond the tag's real data, so the loop reads `length * bytes_per_format` bytes of neighbouring memory into the decoded value list returned to userland. URATIONAL/SRATIONAL read 8 bytes/element (den at `4+(char *)vptr`), extending the over-read.
findings.what_to_confirm: That the caller validated the component count against the IFD value/offset extent so `length` elements of the declared format fit inside the image buffer before this loop runs.

findings.title: Missing `break` after TAG_FMT_SINGLE reads 8 bytes for a 4-byte format
findings.bug_class: Out-of-bounds read via switch fall-through (4-byte format read as 8 bytes)
findings.invariant: A TAG_FMT_SINGLE element must be decoded with a single 4-byte read and then stop; each read must use the per-element cursor `vptr` bounded to the source buffer.
findings.failure_mode: The SINGLE case lacks a terminating `break`, so after `*(float *)value` (4 bytes) it falls through and also executes `*(double *)value` (8 bytes). When `value` points at the final 4 bytes of the image buffer this reads 4 bytes past the end. Compounding it, both float and double dereference the base `value` rather than `vptr`, so array elements past index 0 re-read the first element and the over-read is at a fixed offset regardless of `idex`.
findings.what_to_confirm: Whether upstream ever dispatches format TAG_FMT_SINGLE here, and that a `break;` is added after the float read and both reads use `vptr` with a bounds check.

findings.title: Unbounded scans: php_strnlen and MAKER_NOTE strlen over-read `value`
findings.anchor: length = php_strnlen(value, length); / length = MIN(length, strlen(value));
findings.code:
length = php_strnlen(value, length);
				info_value->s = estrndup(value, length);
findings.bug_class: Out-of-bounds read (terminator scan past buffer end)
findings.invariant: Any NUL-terminator scan over `value` must be bounded by the readable source size, not merely by the attacker `length` (php_strnlen) or unbounded (strlen).
findings.failure_mode: In the STRING branch, `php_strnlen(value, length)` scans up to attacker-chosen `length` bytes; if there is no NUL before the buffer end it over-reads (then estrndup copies the over-read span). In the UNDEFINED branch, `MIN(length, strlen(value))` for TAG_MAKER_NOTE calls `strlen(value)`, an entirely unbounded scan that runs off the end of a non-NUL-terminated maker-note payload before MIN can clamp. Both leak adjacent memory extent/contents.
findings.what_to_confirm: That `value` for string/maker-note tags is guaranteed NUL-terminated within the parsed buffer, or that scans are bounded by the true remaining byte count.

findings.title: Weak input gate: only `length < 0` checked, no buffer-extent validation
findings.bug_class: Missing input validation (length not bounded to source size)
findings.where_it_lives: corpus/train/train_0135_cwe125.c; extent must be supplied/checked in implementation (caller file not provided)
findings.invariant: Before any read of `value`, the function must know and enforce the number of readable bytes behind it; a signed non-negative check is not a bounds check.
findings.failure_mode: The single gate admits any positive `int` `length` (up to INT_MAX), which is then cast `(size_t)length` and used as an element/byte count everywhere below. Because no buffer-length parameter is passed in, this function structurally cannot bound the reads itself — it trusts the caller entirely, which is exactly why the over-reads above are reachable. This is the root enabling condition for findings 1-4.
findings.what_to_confirm: Whether the caller (exif_process_IFD_TAG) validates the component count/byte size against the IFD value/offset before calling, since this function receives no extent.

findings.title: Unchecked `section_index` used as array index and shift amount
findings.where_it_lives: corpus/train/train_0135_cwe125.c; caller decides section_index (file not provided)
findings.invariant: `0 <= section_index < number of sections` (and `< 31` for the `1<<section_index` shift) on every call.
findings.failure_mode: `section_index` indexes `info_list[]` for realloc/append and is used as a shift count at line 126, with no local validation. In normal flow it is a fixed section constant, so this is latent; but any caller that forwards an image-influenced or out-of-range value produces an out-of-bounds write into the section array or undefined-behavior shift.
findings.what_to_confirm: That every caller passes a compile-time-bounded SECTION_* constant and never an attacker-derived index.

audit_checklist: Confirm the caller (exif_process_IFD_TAG / maker-note handlers) validates the tag's component count and byte size against the IFD value/offset extent before invoking exif_iif_add_value, since this function receives no buffer length.
For UNDEFINED/BYTE/SBYTE tags, verify estrndup(value, length) cannot read past the parsed image buffer when length is attacker-maximal.
For the numeric loop, verify length * php_tiff_bytes_per_format[format] (including the 8-byte URATIONAL/SRATIONAL reads) never exceeds the readable source bytes.
Add a break after TAG_FMT_SINGLE and change the float/double reads to use vptr (with a bounds check) instead of the base `value`.
Ensure string/maker-note payloads are NUL-terminated within the buffer or bound php_strnlen/strlen to the true remaining byte count.
Confirm section_index is always a bounded SECTION_* constant (< section count and < 31) at every call site.
Verify php_tiff_bytes_per_format[format] is only indexed with a validated format so the stride table read is in-bounds.
